title: Hydra Approval Record state
description: Durable who/when/why for every human-gated decision. state/approvals.json is a map of subject-key -> per-subject record holding the CURRENT approval status plus an append-only history of every action. Written by scripts/approve.sh, read by scripts/approvals.sh. Pairs with the Decision Record (#267): an approval action's decision_ref links to a Decision Record entry, and that entry's approval_ref links back here (approval:<subject-key>). Spec: docs/specs/2026-06-08-approval-record.md (ticket #268). Runtime-gitignored like the other state/*.json files; state/approvals.example.json is the committed shape.

action.description: One recorded approval action — also the shape of `current` and each `history[]` entry.

allOf.description: WHO audit invariant: an approved/rejected action MUST carry a non-empty approver. Enforced in the durable schema (not only at write time by approve.sh) so an imported/hand-edited/corrupted record cannot defeat the core WHO guarantee.
Mirror invariant: an awaiting action has no human yet, so approver MUST be null. Prevents a misleading record (awaiting by a named person) from passing strict validation.

decision.description: The action taken. `awaiting` = surfaced, no human decision yet.

approver.description: WHO. Non-empty string for approved/rejected (enforced by the allOf/if-then above); null for awaiting (no human yet).

ts.description: WHEN. ISO-8601 UTC timestamp, second precision (e.g. 2026-06-08T18:30:00Z). Component ranges are bounded (month 01-12, day 01-31, hour 00-23, min/sec 00-59) so an impossible value like 2026-99-99T99:99:99Z is rejected.

reason.description: WHY. Non-empty rationale for the action.

decision_ref.description: Link to the Decision Record entry this approves (e.g. merge-surface:tonychang04/hydra#501). Mirror of #267's approval_ref. null when not linked.

approvals.description: Map of subject key -> per-subject approval record. Subject keys are normalized refs (e.g. tonychang04/hydra#501, worker-abc). Keys starting with underscore (e.g. _comment) are meta and ignored by readers.

subject.description: The normalized subject key — echoes the map key.

history.description: Append-only list of every action recorded for this subject, oldest first. The last element equals `current`.
